_schemaDefinition.name: clean display name from Figma page
_schemaDefinition.figma: nodeId (primary component set), fileKey, link, netlifyFallback
_schemaDefinition.primaryComponent: name of the component set selected as primary for this family
_schemaDefinition.subComponents: all component sets in this family — internal: true flags sub-slots
_schemaDefinition.tokens: brand tokens this component uses (hand-authored or from tokens.json)
_schemaDefinition.dos: hand-authored do list — preserved across re-exports
_schemaDefinition.donts: hand-authored dont list — preserved across re-exports

figmaPush.defaultVariant: auto-derived from props; override manually if needed
figmaPush.requiredProps: props that must be explicitly set before commit

components.name: Action Footer
components.description:
A persistent footer bar that anchors to the bottom of the viewport, containing primary actions, payment summaries, and legal agreements for checkout or transactional flows. Use on mobile and desktop purchase screens, booking forms, or any multi-step process where users need to review costs and confirm actions without scrolling away.
Keywords: action footer, sticky footer, bottom bar, checkout footer, payment footer, summary bar, action bar, footer navigation, bottom navigation, persistent footer, Apple HIG toolbar, Material bottom app bar, Fluent command bar, Polaris page actions

components.name: Button
components.description:
Button Component
A versatile button component that supports various states, styles, and sizes to accommodate different user interface needs.


Overview
Buttons allow users to trigger actions or events with a single click. They communicate clickable elements of the interface and can contain text labels and/or icons.


Component Variations


Type Variants
Primary (Solid background)
Secondary (Outlined)
White - Outlined
White - Filled


States
Default
Hover
Pressed/Active
Focused
Disabled
Loading


Sizes
Medium (Default)
Large (Extended)
dos: Use Type=Primary for the single most important CTA per page section
Include Hover and Focus states — required for Web desktop accessibility
Always set a Viewport — Desktop, Tablet, Mobile, or mWeb
Use State=Loading when the action is processing
Set Language=Arabic for RTL page contexts
donts: Do not omit Viewport — all Web components require it
Do not use App Buttons component in Web designs — this is the Web Button
Do not skip Hover state on Web — it is required for desktop pointer interaction
Do not resize below 44px height — WCAG 2.1 touch target minimum
Do not place more than one Primary button per page section

Destination Cards
Carousel Controls

Pagination Indicators (Dots): Visually indicate current position within the carousel.

components.name: Input Field
dos: Include Hover state in Web input — users hover before focusing
Always pair Show Error Message=True with State=Error
Always set Viewport — required for Web components
Use Helper Text to guide formatting (e.g. 'DD/MM/YYYY')
donts: Do not omit Viewport on Web Input Field
Do not show Error Message without State=Error set
Do not confuse Read Only with Disabled — different affordance meanings
Do not use App Input Field in Web designs — use Web Input Field

components.name: Modal

content.name: Description
content.defaultValue: Thinking of travelling somewhere soon? Here are some options to help you get sta

dos: Use --f-brand-color-overlay for the modal backdrop (never a custom rgba)
Include a Close Button and keyboard Escape dismissal in implementation
Use Type=Confirmation for destructive actions that require user acknowledgement
donts: Do not use App Modal Alert on Web pages — use this Web Modal component
Do not stack multiple Modals — only one Modal should be visible at a time
Do not omit the overlay backdrop — it provides focus isolation
Do not use --f-brand-shadow-medium for Modals — large shadow is required

components.name: Progress Tracker
components.description:
A clear, step-by-step visual indicator designed to inform users about their current progress and the status of processes such as refund requests or application workflows.

🔍 Anatomy:
1. Progress Bar
• Visually indicates overall completion.
• Highlights completed, active, pending, or error states with clear colours.
2. Steps
• Icon: Represents the state clearly (completed ✅, active 🔄, pending ⏳, error ❌).
• Label: Concise, informative text describing each step.
• Subtext (optional): Provides additional context, such as estimated completion time or status details.


🌟 States Supported:
• Completed
• Active/In Progress
• Pending
• Error/Rejected


🎯 Best Used For:
• Tracking complex multi-step processes (e.g., refund requests, order processing).
• Keeping users informed and reassured about current statuses and next steps.
• Providing transparency and clear communication to enhance user experience.

components.name: Super Nav
dos: Always use Super Nav as the primary navigation on Web — never build a custom nav
Use Type=Transparent over hero images and full-bleed page headers
Always set Viewport — Super Nav has distinct Desktop vs Mobile layouts
Set Language=Arabic for RTL page contexts
donts: Do not use App Navigation Bar on Web — use Super Nav
Do not omit Super Nav from full-page Web designs
Do not custom-build navigation elements when Super Nav covers the use case

components.name: Text Area
components.description:
Text Area


The Text Area component is designed for collecting longer text input from users. It supports various states, character counting, and helper text functionality. 


The component is fully bi-directional, supporting both English and Arabic languages, with appropriate text alignment and UI element positioning.
